name: Weather
description: Tornadoes, hurricanes, clouds, climate — meteorology trivia.

questions.q: What is the difference between weather and climate?
choices: Weather is short-term; climate is long-term
Weather is hot; climate is cold
Weather is local; climate is global

questions.q: What wind speed (sustained) does a storm need to be classified as a hurricane?

source.notes: Saffir-Simpson Cat 1 starts at 74 mph (~119 km/h).

questions.q: What is the strongest tier on the Saffir-Simpson Hurricane Wind Scale?
What scale rates tornado intensity?

source.notes: EF0-EF5; replaced original Fujita Scale in 2007.

questions.q: What cloud type is associated with thunderstorms and produces hail?
Which cloud type appears as thin, wispy 'mare's tails' high in the sky?

source.notes: Highest clouds, made of ice crystals.

questions.q: Which atmospheric phenomenon causes a rainbow?
What U.S. region is known as 'Tornado Alley'?
choices: Central Great Plains (TX, OK, KS, NE)

source.notes: Includes Texas, Oklahoma, Kansas, Nebraska — extending into Iowa, Missouri, SD.

questions.q: What is the name of the thin layer of frozen rain droplets known as 'frozen rain'?

source.notes: Sleet = ice pellets that freeze before hitting ground; freezing rain = liquid that freezes on impact.

questions.q: What instrument measures atmospheric pressure?
What instrument measures wind speed?
What is the name of the boundary where two air masses meet, often producing storms?

source.notes: Cold front, warm front, occluded front, stationary front.

questions.q: What type of front is associated with sudden temperature drops + thunderstorms?

source.notes: Cold air pushes under warm air, lifting it rapidly.

questions.q: What is the term for the stationary 'eye' at the center of a hurricane?
Which deadly Pacific weather phenomenon is the equivalent of an Atlantic hurricane?

source.notes: Same storm, different name by region. Indian Ocean = 'cyclone.'

questions.q: What 1900 hurricane killed 6,000-12,000 people in Texas — the deadliest U.S. natural disaster?

source.notes: September 8, 1900. Galveston, Texas was nearly wiped off the map.

questions.q: Which 2005 hurricane devastated New Orleans?
What is thunder's cause?
choices: Air rapidly expanding from lightning's heat

source.notes: Lightning superheats air to ~30,000K, causing rapid expansion → shockwave.

questions.q: Approximately how fast does sound (thunder) travel through air?

source.notes: ~1,125 ft/s. Count 5 sec between flash and thunder = 1 mile away.

questions.q: Which 1859 solar storm — strong enough to send sparks from telegraph wires — is called the largest solar event in recorded history?

source.notes: September 1-2, 1859. Named after astronomer Richard Carrington.
